title: Legal Contract Classifier: 94% Cost Reduction via Distillation
subtitle: Knowledge distillation from GPT-4 to Phi-4 Mini for contract classification
description: A legal tech company was using GPT-4 to classify and extract key clauses from contracts  effective, but expensive at $22K/month. We used GPT-4 itself as a teacher to generate a labeled training dataset of 60,000 classified contracts, then fine-tuned a Phi-4 Mini model on the resulting data. The distilled model matches GPT-4's classification accuracy on their contract types at 6% of the per-token cost.

portfolio.projectSummary:
Knowledge distillation is the most underutilized optimization technique in production AI. The idea: use a powerful frontier model (the teacher) to label your data, then train a small model (the student) to replicate the teacher's behavior on your specific task. The student never needs to be generally intelligent  it just needs to be excellent at your one job. For this legal client, GPT-4 was already classifying and extracting clauses from contracts effectively, but at $22K/month  and because it ran as a cloud API, it couldn't be used for the air-gapped client environments a chunk of their business required.

We used GPT-4 as the teacher to label 60,000 contract samples across 14 clause types, driving accuracy with structured chain-of-thought prompting and a 5% manual review pass in which low-confidence labels were regenerated with additional context  bringing final label accuracy to 99.1%. We then fine-tuned Phi-4 Mini on the resulting dataset using QLoRA and quantized it to INT4 with GPTQ so it would fit on modest, self-hosted hardware.

The distilled model reached 98.2% classification accuracy versus GPT-4's 98.7% on the same held-out test set, while running at $0.04/M tokens instead of $15/M. Monthly AI spend dropped from $22,000 to $1,320, and the client now owns a model they can deploy on-premises  including the air-gapped environments a cloud API dependency had previously ruled out. The full engagement took five weeks.

challenges.title: Label Quality from the Teacher
challenges.description: Distillation is only as good as the teacher's labels. We used structured prompting with chain-of-thought to maximize GPT-4 label accuracy, then ran a 5% manual review sample. Labels with confidence below a threshold were regenerated with additional context. Final label accuracy: 99.1%.

challenges.title: Handling Out-of-Distribution Contract Types
challenges.description: The client regularly receives contracts from new jurisdictions and in new formats. We fine-tuned the model on a deliberately diverse set including edge cases, and built a confidence-scoring layer that routes low-confidence predictions to a human reviewer queue rather than auto-classifying them.

challenges.title: Shrinking the Model for On-Prem Hardware
challenges.description: The whole point of distillation here was a model the client could run themselves  including in air-gapped environments with limited hardware. We quantized Phi-4 Mini to INT4 using GPTQ, shrinking its memory and compute footprint enough to fit that infrastructure while holding classification accuracy at 98.2%, statistically on par with the full-precision model.

approach.title: Teacher Labeling with GPT-4
approach.description: We used GPT-4 as the teacher to label 60,000 contracts across 14 clause types. Structured chain-of-thought prompting pushed the teacher to reason through each classification rather than guess, which directly raises the ceiling on what the student model can learn.

approach.title: Label Quality Assurance
approach.description: Distillation inherits the teacher's mistakes, so labels weren't taken on faith. A 5% sample was manually reviewed, and any label below a confidence threshold was regenerated with additional context. Final label accuracy reached 99.1% before a single training step ran.

approach.title: Student Fine-Tuning with QLoRA
approach.description: We fine-tuned Phi-4 Mini on the distilled dataset using QLoRA, which makes training a small model fast and memory-efficient. The training set deliberately over-weighted edge cases and diverse jurisdictions so the student would generalize beyond the common contract types.

approach.title: INT4 Quantization for Deployment
approach.description: We quantized the fine-tuned model to INT4 with GPTQ so it fits self-hosted and air-gapped hardware. Quantization was validated against the held-out test set to confirm the compression didn't cost meaningful accuracy.

approach.title: Confidence Routing Layer
approach.description: Rather than auto-classifying everything, the system scores each prediction and routes low-confidence cases  typically new jurisdictions or unusual formats  to a human reviewer queue, keeping accuracy high on the long tail without a frontier model in the loop.

technicalSolution.title: Distillation Pipeline
technicalSolution.description: The architecture is a clean teacher-student pipeline: GPT-4 generates a labeled dataset, Phi-4 Mini learns to replicate the teacher's behavior on the 14 clause types. The student never needs to be generally intelligent  only excellent at this one classification task  which is exactly why a 14B-class frontier model can be replaced by a few-billion-parameter one.

technicalSolution.title: Quantized On-Prem Serving
technicalSolution.description: The INT4-quantized Phi-4 Mini is served via vLLM on the client's own (Azure or on-prem) infrastructure. Because nothing leaves their environment, the same model can run in fully air-gapped deployments that a GPT-4 API integration could never satisfy.

technicalSolution.title: Accuracy Guardrails
technicalSolution.description: Quality is measured, not assumed: a held-out test set confirms parity (98.2% vs. GPT-4's 98.7%), and the runtime confidence-scoring layer escalates uncertain predictions to humans, so the rare out-of-distribution contract never gets silently misclassified.

useCases.title: Clause Classification
useCases.description: Classifies contracts across 14 clause types at accuracy statistically on par with GPT-4, at a fraction of the per-token cost.

useCases.title: Key Clause Extraction
useCases.description: Surfaces the specific clauses legal teams need to review from each contract, turning a manual read-through into a targeted check.

useCases.title: New-Jurisdiction Handling
useCases.description: Diverse, edge-case-weighted training plus confidence routing keeps accuracy steady on contracts from new jurisdictions and unfamiliar formats.

useCases.title: Air-Gapped Deployment
useCases.description: Runs entirely on the client's own hardware, enabling on-prem and air-gapped use for environments where cloud APIs are not permitted.
